whatThisIs: Look up HCPCS Level I (CPT) and Level II modifiers - the two-character suffixes that change how a procedure code is paid or interpreted. Sophie Well lists modifier 25 (significant separately identifiable E/M), 59 and the X-series subset modifiers (XE, XS, XP, XU), the LT/RT laterality pair, the GA/GX/GY/GZ ABN family, the telehealth 95 modifier, the anesthesia physical-status modifiers (P1-P6), and every other active code with the official CMS short description.
whenToUse: Use this when a claim line is denied for a missing or wrong modifier, when you need to decide between 59 and the X-series subset modifiers on a same-day procedure pair, or when a clinician asks why an E/M plus procedure on the same date needs the 25 modifier on the E/M side. Modifiers carry significant payment and audit weight - the wrong one can trigger a payer takeback months later - so verifying the exact short description before submission is reference-table work that pays for itself.
